narrative.executive_view: Payroll processing has abundant AI/RPA task exposure, but local bureaus face scale platforms and embedded fintech. Regulatory liability and money movement preserve an intermediary, though often the platform rather than the acquirable bureau [S1].
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Payroll platforms are investing in generative AI and automate routine compliance, calculation and client workflows [S1]. Human exception handling, tax notices and client relationships remain; the supplied CPA-heavy occupation mix omits payroll clerks and is a material proxy caveat.
narrative.value_capture: PEPM recurring pricing can retain automation gains, but ADP’s recurring-revenue platform, long retention and client-funds capabilities show the scale advantage local bureaus face [S1]. Competitive platforms can pass savings through, so capture is moderate rather than assumed.
narrative.adoption_timing: Platform payroll is already digital and ADP reports generative-AI investment [S1]. Effective automation is estimated above 50%, so t50 is zero, although independent bureaus may lag the platforms.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The deterministic screen has 297 target-band firms, but small bureaus face fixed compliance costs and channel dependence. Active platform consolidation is not used as AI-adoption evidence and multiples remain low-confidence.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid payroll intermediation is durable because multi-state remittance, custody, reporting-agent responsibility and exception handling remain costly. The local bureau is pressured by platform oligopoly, which is distinct from the survival of payroll processing.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Labor share is unusually high and compares different vintages; revenue can net client funds. The supplied 5412 mix is not payroll-specific. No reopened independent-bureau price or transaction census supports precise capture or multiples.

sources.title: ADP Fiscal 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Recurring payroll platform, retention, regulatory context and generative-AI investment.

sources.title: Paychex Fiscal 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Payroll/HCM regulatory complexity and platform scope.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Processing and support automate heavily; supplied CPA proxy constrains weights.
caveats: Payroll clerks absent from supplied 5412 proxy.

pi_dist.rationale: Subscription pricing retains some savings, offset by platform competition.

pi_moat.rationale: Compliance and client data help, but platforms own scale.
caveats: Platform evidence is not local-bureau evidence.

t50_years.rationale: Digitized platforms with AI investment imply above-50% effective automation.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Platform evidence supports an above-50% estimate.

historical_analogs.value: SaaS payroll automation retains compliance intermediaries.

buy_mult.rationale: Moderate recurring revenue but platform risk.

terminal_value.justification: Compliance preserves processing but scale platforms pressure local bureaus [S1].

dataset_mismatch.description: CPA-heavy role mix omits payroll clerks.
